title: Purchase Department
learning_objectives: Understand the role of the Purchase Department.
Understand supplier selection and vendor management.
Recognize how purchasing supports uninterrupted production.
Understand the use of SAP MM in procurement activities.

introduction.title: What is the Purchase Department?
introduction.content: The Purchase Department is responsible for procuring raw materials, spare parts, consumables, equipment and services required for business operations. It ensures that the right materials are purchased at the right quality, quantity, price and delivery time while maintaining strong supplier relationships.

business_purpose.title: Why is the Purchase Department Important?
business_purpose.content: Efficient procurement ensures uninterrupted production, controls costs and maintains product quality. Delays in purchasing or poor supplier management can directly impact manufacturing schedules and customer deliveries.

key_responsibilities.description: Purchase raw materials, spare parts and services.
Evaluate and maintain relationships with suppliers.
Negotiate competitive pricing and favorable terms.
Coordinate with Warehouse to maintain stock availability.
Ensure procurement follows company policies and regulations.

coordination_with_departments.purpose: Procures raw materials based on production plans.
Coordinates receipt and storage of purchased materials.
Ensures purchased materials meet quality standards.
Processes supplier payments and budget approvals.
Purchases spare parts and maintenance materials.
Records procurement transactions and inventory updates.

business_example.title: Purchase Department at JSL
business_example.content: The Production Department plans a new manufacturing schedule and raises a Purchase Requisition for stainless steel raw material. The Purchase Department evaluates approved suppliers, compares quotations, creates a Purchase Order in SAP MM and tracks delivery. Once the material arrives, Warehouse receives it, Quality inspects it and Finance processes the supplier invoice.

important_notes: Timely procurement directly affects production continuity.
Strong supplier relationships improve business reliability.
Cost should never compromise material quality.
Accurate procurement records support audits and compliance.
